title: 10 best herbal creams for gastroparesis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain gingerols and shogaols, bioactive compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal discomfort. Additionally, ginger has been shown to increase the motility of the stomach and small intestine, helping to improve digestion and reduce food stagnation. By reducing inflammation and promoting digestive motility, herbal ginger creams can provide relief from gastroparesis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which may contribute to gastroparesis symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

Digestive stimulant properties: The natural oils and extracts present in herbal ginger creams, such as ginger oil and turmeric, can stimulate digestion and improve gastric motility, helping to alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis like bloating, nausea, and slow stomach emptying.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds can help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to gastroparesis symptoms like abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing nausea and discomfort associated with gastroparesis.

Rhyzomes: As the main source of ginger's medicinal properties, rhyzomes are rich in sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and terpenoids that help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as bloating and abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried ginger root, 500ml of distilled water, and 250g of beeswax.
Steam-distill the dried ginger root in boiling water for 30 minutes, then filter the resulting liquid.
Mix the filtered liquid with 200g of coconut oil, 50g of shea butter, and 10g of vitamin e oil.
Melt the beeswax by heating it in a double boiler for 5 minutes, then combine it with the mixture.
Whip the mixture using an electric mixer until it thickens into a smooth cream, taking approximately 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with gastroparesis because they have a calming effect on the muscles of the digestive tract, allowing for easier movement of food through the system. The menthol in peppermint also relaxes the stomach and reduces muscle spasms that can lead to nausea and vomiting. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the gut, further alleviating symptoms associated with gastroparesis. By soothing and calming the digestive tract, peppermint creams provide relief from gastroparesis-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Irritant properties: Peppermint's irritant properties help stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of gastroparesis, such as bloating and discomfort, by increasing gut motility.
Antispasmodic effects: The antispasmodic compounds in peppermint cream help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with gastroparesis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, such as abdominal pain and nausea.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of menthol, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh peppermint leaves mentha x piperita and wash thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the peppermint leaves in a dehydrator at 30°c for 2 hours or air dry for 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried peppermint leaves with 200ml of coconut oil and 100ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler until the beeswax melts completely, stirring occasionally over 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass jar, allowing it to cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets the underlying mechanisms of this condition. Curcumin has been shown to improve gut motility and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with gastroparesis. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties may help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the stomach lining, further supporting its therapeutic benefits for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms of gastroparesis.

COX-2 inhibition: Curcumin's ability to inhibit COX-2 enzyme production reduces prostaglandin synthesis, leading to decreased gastric acid secretion and inflammation, which can help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with gastroparesis.

Gastroprotective activity: Turmeric has been shown to have gastroprotective properties, including reducing gastric ulcers and mucosal damage, which can help prevent complications such as bleeding and perforation in individuals with gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, the roots of the turmeric plant are extracted and used to create creams that help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis by reducing inflammation and improving digestion.

Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the turmeric plant, also known as rhyzomes, contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which are responsible for the anti-inflammatory properties that benefit individuals with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried turmeric powder containing 95% curcuminoids and mix it with three tablespoons of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for five minutes or until the oil is warm.
Add two drops of peppermint essential oil and one tablespoon of beeswax pellets to the warm oil mixture.
Whisk the mixture continuously for five minutes or until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has thickened.
Pour the curcuma longa cream into a sterilized jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to six weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps soothe digestive issues associated with gastroparesis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhizae radix, also known as licorice root, for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Combine the licorice root with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 60°c before adding 10 milliliters of glycerin and 5 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours before use, allowing the ingredients to mature and blend together effectively.

remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated, allowing them to dry completely within 24 hours.
Grind dried foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, obtaining 30 grams of the powdered herb.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes, allowing the mixture to infuse and combine.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining 50 milliliters of the herbal infusion.

Combine the herbal infusion with 20 grams of beeswax pellets in a double boiler setup over low heat, stirring constantly until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted, resulting in a smooth cream consistency after approximately 15 minutes preparation time.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves from an organic farm or your own garden in the morning after dew has evaporated.

Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, squeezing out as much oil as possible from the herbal mixture.

Strain the heated mixture through cheesecloth to separate the infused oil from the solids then discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with 30g of beeswax and heat until melted and well combined.
Pour the melted mixture into small jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with gastroparesis because they can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of delayed gastric emptying. The fatty acid content in saw palmetto has been shown to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can exacerbate muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines. By blocking this conversion, saw palmetto creams may help reduce muscle spasms and improve digestion in individuals with gastroparesis. This natural approach can provide relief from symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Saw palmetto has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
Digestive soothe: The herb has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help ease cramping, bloating, and other uncomfortable symptoms associated with gastroparesis.

Muscle relaxant: Saw palmetto's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue in the digestive tract may help improve gastric motility (the movement of food through the stomach) and reduce the severity of gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Saw palmetto roots contain flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate gastrointestinal symptoms associated with gastroparesis.
Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including gastroparesis, due to their ability to improve gut motility and relieve symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried serenoa repens leaves measure out 20 grams for this recipe.
Combine dried serenoa repens leaves with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil.
Add 30 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the mixture stir well.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 20 minutes or until it reaches 65 degrees celsius.
Remove from heat let cool to room temperature then whip until creamy texture forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain isoflavones, specifically formononetin and biochanin A, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. This relief from muscle spasms and inflammation can help ease symptoms of gastroparesis, such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Additionally, the creams may also improve gut motility by reducing smooth muscle contractions and increasing peristalsis, allowing for more efficient digestion and elimination of waste.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover cream contains isoflavones, which have been shown to improve gut motility and reduce symptoms of gastroparesis by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines.

Anthocyanins: The anthocyanins present in red clover cream have antioxidant properties that help protect the gut lining from inflammation and damage, reducing the risk of gastroparesis and related symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Fiber content: Red clover cream contains a type of fiber called pectin, which can help regulate bowel movements and improve digestion in individuals with gastroparesis by providing bulk to stool and preventing constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in isoflavones, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with gastroparesis.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, vitamins, and antioxidants, which can help to lubricate the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried trifolium pratense flowers and chop them into small pieces.
Combine chopped flowers with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax, vitamin e oil, and other ingredients such as aloe vera gel.
Pour the cooled mixture into glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that soothe the stomach lining and relax digestive muscles. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce inflammation and spasms in the stomach, allowing food to move more efficiently through the digestive system. This relief from discomfort and inflammation can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, including nausea, vomiting, bloating, and abdominal pain, promoting a healthier and more balanced digestive system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as bloating, abdominal pain, and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Yarrow cream's antioxidant property helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage the stomach lining and worsen gastroparesis symptoms, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing oxidative stress.

Stomach-soothing property: Yarrow cream's stomach-soothing property helps to calm the digestive system, reducing spasms and contractions that can impede digestion and exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with gastroparesis.
Seeds: Containing natural oils that promote gut health, reduce symptoms of nausea and vomiting, and provide relief from abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried achillea millefolium leaves from a trusted source.
Measure out 20 grams of dried leaves and steep them in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup, discarding solids.
Combine the resulting infusion with 50 grams of beeswax, 25 grams of coconut oil, and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler, heating until melted.
Remove from heat, let cool to around 60 degrees celsius, and whip the mixture until it thickens into a smooth cream.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for gastroparesis are topical ointments containing natural ingredients derived from plants, which help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis by soothing digestive discomfort and reducing inflammation. These creams can be applied directly to the abdomen, providing quick relief from symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and nausea. For example, creams containing marshmallow root or aloe vera have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, improving digestion and overall quality of life for individuals with gastroparesis.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gastroparesis with herbal creams is to use a combination of anti-inflammatory and digestive soothing herbs. Look for creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and peppermint, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort. Apply the cream topically to the abdomen or as directed by a healthcare professional to help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with gastroparesis.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastroparesis that are treated with herbal creams are irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and leaky gut syndrome. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and licorice root can help soothe digestive discomforts such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. These natural remedies can also reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from symptoms similar to those experienced with gastroparesis.
